title: Accessibility Features
subtitle: Technology for Everyone!
overview: This session introduces students to accessibility features in digital devices that help people with visual, hearing, physical, or learning challenges use technology easily and independently.

images._note: Add real classroom activity photos, accessibility tool demos, screen reader usage, magnifier examples, captions demo etc.

gallery.caption: Screen Reader helps visually impaired users hear text aloud
Magnifier zooms text and images for better visibility
Voice Typing converts speech into text
Closed Captions help users understand audio content

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Braille keyboard or accessibility keyboard photo
gallery.caption: Special keyboards help differently-abled users interact with devices

keyTopics: Voice Typing — speak instead of typing
High Contrast Mode — easy reading display

youtubeVideos.title: What are Accessibility Features?
youtubeVideos._note: Replace with actual YouTube video ID if needed
youtubeVideos.desc: A beginner-friendly introduction to accessibility tools and inclusive technology.

youtubeVideos.title: How Screen Readers Work
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how visually impaired users navigate computers using screen readers.

youtubeVideos.title: Voice Typing Tutorial
youtubeVideos.desc: Learn how speech can be converted into text using voice typing tools.

youtubeVideos.title: Accessibility Features in Smartphones
youtubeVideos.desc: Explore useful accessibility settings available in modern smartphones.

activity.title: Try Accessibility Features on Computer or Mobile
steps: Turn ON Screen Reader or Narrator
Try Magnifier to zoom the screen
Use Voice Typing and speak a sentence
Enable Closed Captions on a video
Observe how accessibility helps different users

quiz.q: What is the purpose of Accessibility Features?
options: To help everyone use technology easily
quiz.explanation: Accessibility features help all people, including differently-abled users, use technology comfortably.

quiz.q: Which feature reads text aloud on a computer or phone?
quiz.explanation: A Screen Reader converts on-screen text into spoken audio.

quiz.q: What does a Magnifier tool do?
quiz.explanation: Magnifier enlarges screen content for users with low vision.
Voice Typing converts spoken words into written text.

options: Watching videos silently or helping hearing-impaired users
quiz.explanation: Closed captions display spoken words as text on the screen.

quiz.q: High Contrast Mode helps users by:
quiz.explanation: High contrast improves visibility between text and background.

quiz.q: Which accessibility feature helps people who cannot type easily?
quiz.explanation: Voice Typing allows users to speak instead of typing.

options: People with different needs and abilities
quiz.explanation: Accessibility tools are designed for inclusive technology use.

quiz.q: Which feature converts written text into spoken words?
quiz.explanation: Text-to-Speech technology reads written text aloud.

options: Ensuring everyone can access and use technology
quiz.explanation: Digital Inclusion means technology should be accessible for everyone.

quiz.q: Which accessibility feature is commonly used by visually impaired users?
quiz.explanation: Screen Readers help visually impaired users navigate devices through audio.
Captions display spoken dialogue and sounds as text.

quiz.q: Which device commonly includes accessibility settings?
quiz.explanation: Most modern digital devices provide accessibility features.
Speech-to-Text technology converts spoken language into text.

options: Because technology should be usable by everyone
quiz.explanation: Accessibility ensures equal opportunities for learning and technology use.
